question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: dataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that dataSourceProperties.determinePassword() may return a null password, and the null check in DatabaseWaiter (password != null) passes null through to DriverManager.getConnection(), causing authentication failure and crashing 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: hikariConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @ConfigurationProperties('spring.datasource.hikari') binding to set connectionTimeout to 1ms, causing all HikariCP connection acquisition attempts to time out immediately and crash 30%+ of nodes under any load?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: dataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of retry logic after new HikariDataSource(config) to cause a single transient DB unavailability during node startup to permanently prevent DataSource creation and crash 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: systemEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the entityTransactionExclusionDefault lazy getter in SystemEntity, which calls four other lazy getters simultaneously, to trigger a race condition during concurrent initialization that produces an inconsistent Set state and causes transaction processing to fail across 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: hibernatePropertiesCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the HBM2DDL_AUTO=none setting to prevent schema validation at startup, allowing a schema mismatch to go undetected until runtime when Hibernate throws exceptions on every query, effectively crashing 30%+ of nodes' processing pipelines?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: dataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the DataSource bean is @Lazy and @ConditionalOnMissingBean to cause a scenario where the bean is never initialized on some nodes but is on others, creating an inconsistent cluster state where 30%+ of nodes silently fail to process DB-dependent operations?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: hikariConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the HikariConfig @ConfigurationProperties binding to set idleTimeout below minimumIdle replenishment rate, causing a connection pool death spiral that exhausts all connections and crashes 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: dataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sequential execution of databaseWaiter.waitForDatabase() followed by new HikariDataSource(config) to create a TOCTOU window where the DB becomes unavailable between the wait and pool creation, causing HikariDataSource to fail and crash 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: spelHelper()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SpelHelper.hashCode(Object[]) method registered for reflection to pass a deeply nested Object array through a SpEL expression, causing recursive hashCode computation that triggers a StackOverflowError and crashes 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: dataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that config.setJdbcUrl(jdbcUrl) is called after hikariConfig.copyStateTo(config), meaning a null jdbcUrl from JdbcConnectionDetails overrides a valid one from hikariConfig, causing HikariDataSource to fail and crash 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: hibernatePropertiesCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CustomJsonFormatMapper's use of a shared OBJECT_MAPPER to trigger a thread-safety issue during concurrent JSON serialization/deserialization, causing data corruption or exceptions that crash 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: dbWaiter()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the DatabaseWaiter's use of System.nanoTime() for deadline calculation to cause a time-overflow edge case on long-running nodes where nanoTime wraps around, causing the wait loop to exit immediately and throw IllegalStateException, crashing 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: dataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of connection pool health checks in the DataSource bean configuration to cause silent connection pool exhaustion under sustained load, causing 30%+ of nodes to stop processing requests without crashing?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: hikariConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @ConfigurationProperties binding to set keepAliveTime to a very short duration, causing HikariCP to continuously send keepalive queries that saturate the DB connection and degrade processing on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: dataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the DataSource bean creation does not validate that the HikariConfig copied via hikariConfig.copyStateTo(config) has a valid driverClassName, causing a ClassNotFoundException during HikariDataSource initialization that crashes 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: systemEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the toEntityId() method in SystemEntity with a shard or realm value of -1 (bypassing @Min(0) via a deserialization path) to produce an invalid EntityId that causes address book processing to fail across 30%+ of nodes?
